description.en: A rustic 70/30 rye-and-wheat sourdough loaf, the everyday bread of German households. Rye gives it backbone and long keeping; wheat softens the crumb. A stale-bread soaker deepens the flavor further.
description.de: Ein rustikales Mischbrot aus Roggen und Weizen im Verhältnis etwa 70:30, mit Sauerteig geführt. Der Roggen bringt Kraft und Haltbarkeit, der Weizen macht die Krume milder. Ein Altbrot-Quellstück vertieft das Aroma zusätzlich.
description.es: Un pan mixto rústico de centeno y trigo, en proporción aproximada 70:30, fermentado con masa madre. El centeno aporta cuerpo y larga conservación; el trigo suaviza la miga. Un remojo de pan viejo profundiza aún más el sabor.
description.fr: Un pain mixte rustique de seigle et de blé, dans une proportion d'environ 70:30, au levain. Le seigle apporte du corps et une bonne conservation, le blé adoucit la mie. Un trempage de vieux pain approfondit encore la saveur.

origin.note: German bread law defines Roggenmischbrot as containing 50–89% rye flour with the remainder wheat; below that it is Weizenmischbrot instead.

ingredients.note: rye flour type 1150, split between sourdough and main dough
wheat type 1050 plus whole wheat flour, autolysed

label.fr: vieux pain séché et torréfié, moulu (Altbrot)

ingredients.note: soaked as a flavor and moisture-boosting Quellstück
optional, for a more reliable rise alongside the sourdough

keeping.note: Wrap in linen or paper; the crumb stays moist for most of a week.

summary.en: The evening before, mix rye flour, warm water and a spoonful of active starter into a sourdough and let it ripen about 20 hours at room temperature.
In parallel, soak toasted, ground stale bread in warm salted water for 8–12 hours; this Altbrot soaker adds moisture and a deeper, rounder flavor.
Rest the wheat and whole wheat flour with warm water for an hour so it fully hydrates before mixing.
Combine the ripe sourdough, the bread soaker, the autolysed wheat, the remaining rye flour and water, and a little fresh yeast for insurance. Mix briefly to a smooth, warm dough.
Let the dough rest about 40 minutes, then shape first into a round and then into an oval loaf.
Proof seam-up in a floured banneton for a little over an hour.
Bake hot with plenty of steam, starting around 250°C and dropping to 200°C, for about 45 minutes total.

source.adaptation: Simplified from a three-stage recipe (sourdough, Altbrot soaker, autolyse); Altbrot's water was folded into the hydration total, its dry weight kept out of the flour percentages.
